message:
feat(abi): port fchmodat2 + eventfd2 + 4 rt_sig* (rt_sigprocmask/queueinfo/suspend/tgsigqueueinfo) (bd-dea1g)

Six more Linux libc syscall wrappers, all forwarding via libc::syscall
+ raw_syscall_with_errno bridge:

  - fchmodat2(dirfd, pathname, mode, flags) (Linux 6.6+,
    SYS_fchmodat2=452): like fchmodat but accepts AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW
    + AT_EMPTY_PATH.
  - eventfd2(initval, flags) (SYS_eventfd2=290): kernel-name variant
    of eventfd that some sandbox/seccomp policies trace explicitly.
  - rt_sigprocmask(how, *set, *oldset, sigsetsize)
    (SYS_rt_sigprocmask=14).
  - rt_sigqueueinfo(tgid, sig, *uinfo) (SYS_rt_sigqueueinfo=129):
    send a siginfo_t to a thread group.
  - rt_sigsuspend(*mask, sigsetsize) (SYS_rt_sigsuspend=130).
  - rt_tgsigqueueinfo(tgid, tid, sig, *uinfo)
    (SYS_rt_tgsigqueueinfo=297): send a siginfo_t to a specific
    thread.

Skipped on purpose: rt_sigaction (kernel struct k_sigaction layout
differs from libc's struct sigaction — needs adapter glue, not just a
forward) and rt_sigreturn (kernel signal-trampoline, never callable
from user code).

Tests: 6 abi tests covering fchmodat2 mode-change round-trip on a
real temp file, eventfd2 fd creation + initval read-back, rt_sigprocmask
SIG_BLOCK then SIG_SETMASK round-trip on SIGUSR1, and EFAULT NULL
guards across rt_sigqueueinfo / rt_sigsuspend / rt_tgsigqueueinfo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
